title: Change Event Queue and Dispatch Flow
interaction_flow: --- PRODUCER SIDE (enqueue) ---
ProducerThread → EventDispatcher: dispatchDataChangeEvent(partition, dataCollectionId, changeRecordEmitter)
EventDispatcher → DataCollectionFilter: filter.isIncluded(dataCollectionId) [check include/exclude list]
EventDispatcher → ChangeRecordEmitter: emitChangeRecords(dataCollectionSchema, receiver)
ChangeRecordEmitter → StreamingChangeRecordReceiver: changeRecord(partition, schema, op, key, value, offset, headers)
StreamingChangeRecordReceiver → TopicNamingStrategy: dataChangeTopic(dataCollectionId) [resolve Kafka topic name]
StreamingChangeRecordReceiver → PostProcessorRegistry: doPostProcessing(key, value) [optional SMT-like processing]
StreamingChangeRecordReceiver → ChangeEventCreator: createDataChangeEvent(SourceRecord)
StreamingChangeRecordReceiver → ChangeEventQueue: enqueue(DataChangeEvent)
ChangeEventQueue → ChangeEventQueue: doEnqueue(record) [acquire ReentrantLock]
ChangeEventQueue [BACKPRESSURE]: if queue.size >= maxQueueSize OR currentQueueSizeInBytes >= maxQueueSizeInBytes → signal isFull, await isNotFull(pollInterval)
ChangeEventQueue → QueueProvider: queue.enqueue(record) [actual internal queue insert]
ChangeEventQueue: if batch threshold OR byte threshold reached → signal isFull [wake up consumer early]
ChangeEventQueue → ChangeEventQueue: unlock
--- TOMBSTONE (on DELETE) ---
StreamingChangeRecordReceiver → ChangeEventQueue: enqueue(DataChangeEvent[tombstone]) [if emitTombstonesOnDelete=true]
--- CONSUMER SIDE (poll) ---
ConsumerThread → ChangeEventQueue: poll() [Kafka Connect polling loop]
ChangeEventQueue: acquire lock, set Timer(min(pollInterval, RETURN_CONTROL_INTERVAL))
ChangeEventQueue: loop → drainRecords(records, maxBatchSize) → QueueProvider.poll()
ChangeEventQueue [WAIT]: if batch not full AND bytes below threshold AND timer not expired → signal isNotFull, await isFull(remainingTimeout)
ChangeEventQueue: release lock → return List<DataChangeEvent> [up to maxBatchSize]
ConsumerThread → Kafka: forward records to Kafka broker
--- BENCHMARK CLASSES (performance measurement) ---
ProducerPerf: setup() → builds ChangeEventQueue via Builder, spawns background consumer thread calling poll() in loop
ProducerPerf → ChangeEventQueue: benchmarkProducer() calls enqueue(EVENT) [measures producer throughput, ops/sec]
ConsumerPerf: setup() → builds ChangeEventQueue via Builder, spawns background producer thread calling enqueue() in loop
ConsumerPerf → ChangeEventQueue: benchmarkConsumer() calls poll() [measures consumer throughput, ops/sec]
QueuePerf: benchmarkChangeEventQueue() → starts producer thread (10M enqueue calls) + consumer thread (polls until 10M records received), joins both [measures round-trip latency ms]
MultiWriterQueuePerf: benchmarkChangeEventQueue() → starts 10 producer threads (1M enqueue each) + 1 consumer thread (polls until 10M records), joins all [multi-writer contention test]
key_relationships: ChangeEventQueue<T extends Sizeable> holds QueueProvider<T> (strategy for underlying queue impl)
ChangeEventQueue uses DefaultQueueProvider<T> by default (via Builder if queueProvider not set)
ChangeEventQueue uses ReentrantLock + two Conditions (isFull, isNotFull) for producer-consumer coordination
EventDispatcher contains StreamingChangeRecordReceiver (inner class) — calls queue.enqueue()
EventDispatcher contains BufferingSnapshotChangeRecordReceiver (inner class) — calls queue.enqueue() with buffered swap
EventDispatcher contains IncrementalSnapshotChangeRecordReceiver (inner class) — calls queue.enqueue()
EventDispatcher contains SchemaChangeEventReceiver (inner class) — calls enqueueSchemaChangeMessage() → queue.enqueue()
ChangeEventQueuePerf outer class contains ProducerPerf, ConsumerPerf, QueuePerf, MultiWriterQueuePerf as static inner JMH benchmark classes
All benchmark classes use ChangeEventQueue.Builder<DataChangeEvent> for construction

backpressure_mechanics.enqueue_blocks_when: queue.size() >= maxQueueSize OR (maxQueueSizeInBytes > 0 AND currentQueueSizeInBytes >= maxQueueSizeInBytes)
backpressure_mechanics.enqueue_signals_consumer: isFull.signalAll() when batch threshold OR byte threshold reached

buffering_mode.description: Optional single-cell lookahead buffer via AtomicReference<T>. Used by MySQL connector to set snapshot flags on last record
buffering_mode.enqueue_with_buffering: Swaps new record into bufferedEvent, enqueues the displaced record
buffering_mode.flushBuffer: Applies recordModifier function to buffered event, then enqueues result — used to mark last snapshot record

queue_config_params.queueProvider: DefaultQueueProvider wrapping ArrayDeque or custom QueueProvider impl

important_notes: ChangeEventQueue is the handover point between connector source threads (producers) and the Kafka Connect polling loop (consumer). @ThreadSafe — multiple producers can call enqueue() concurrently (protected by ReentrantLock). poll() is @SingleThreadAccess consumer side. EventDispatcher.StreamingChangeRecordReceiver is the primary path for streaming CDC events. BufferingSnapshotChangeRecordReceiver handles threaded snapshot with synchronized(queue) guard for atomic getAndSet+enqueue.
